title: chargeback — compliance evidence
description: Chargeback module is financial-record-critical (every chargeback is a bank-forced revenue reversal — SOX + tax audit relevant + card network audit relevant) + PII-adjacent (customer_id + submitted_by_user_id captured on every submission + evidence bundles may contain IP address / device fingerprint hash / service attendance records) + advertising-adjacent (feeds negative-value marketing events + fraud model retraining) + card-network-audit-critical (chargeback rate above 0.9% Visa / 1.5% Mastercard triggers merchant-account risk programs). GDPR (Art. 5, 6, 17), CFPB Fair Credit Billing Act, PSD2 (EU), US tax law (7y financial record), SOX 404 (internal controls), SOC 2 CC6.3, PCI-DSS Req 3.4 + 10.5.3 (no PAN storage + audit trail retention), Card Network Rules (VDMP / MDMP / equivalent enforcement).

controls.evidence: Every chargeback records the trigger source (payment_dispute_id when escalated, or webhook_id when direct-from-provider) + submitter of evidence + timestamps + reason code. Full auditability. Bank's decision + rationale captured on won/lost. Customer is transparently informed by their bank; the tenant is transparently informed by us via ChargebackFiledNotification (P0).

Chargeback captures only what the defense computation needs: reference to parent payment + invoice + reason code + amount. Evidence bundle carries service documentation + policy snapshots + communication logs — all pertinent to the disputed transaction. NO card PAN / CVV / magnetic stripe anywhere (enforced by PCI-DSS scan on evidence submission).
7-year retention window justified by SOX + tax + card network audit obligations. Enterprise 10-year extension for regulated tenants. Automated purge via PurgeExpiredChargebackDataJob.

Chargeback processing is contractual — the tenant/customer agreement + card network merchant agreement both mandate chargeback handling. Terms of chargeback + evidence submission + acceptance workflow constitute the contract.

Fraud prevention + merchant-account preservation are legitimate business interests. Rate monitoring + fraud-model retraining feedback loops fall under this basis. Balancing test documented in compliance/chargeback-legitimate-interest-assessment.md.

UserErased (as submitter of evidence) triggers RedactCustomerFromChargebacksOnUserErased — 90 days post-erasure, redact submitted_by_user_id references to a hashed placeholder. Row survives for the 7-year financial audit trail (financial-obligation carve-out per Art. 17 §3(b)). Customer PII within evidence bundles (IP address, device fingerprint hash) is hashed at collection; no reversible PII is stored.

No fully-automated decisions with legal effect. Chargeback outcome is decided by the BANK (external decision-maker) — this module records the outcome. The RDR path is human-initiated (admin clicks accept); rate threshold alerts are informational (do not auto-close tenant accounts).

Chargeback is IMMUTABLE post-terminal. Evidence rows are immutable at creation. Customer-scope middleware NOT needed (no customer routes exist — chargebacks are admin-only). Confidential financial fields (amount_cents, fee_cents, provider_metadata) require chargebacks.view-financials permission. Evidence bundles require chargebacks.view-evidence permission (segregated from view-financials).

Chargebacks are customer-initiated + bank-processed — the tenant cannot prevent them. This module does NOT surface chargeback state to the customer (some card networks explicitly prohibit surfacing). This module does NOT permit merchant retaliation against a customer who filed a chargeback — no customer-visible flag / restriction based on chargeback history. Chargeback outcome does not affect the customer's account standing on the tenant's product surface.

controls.principle: customer authentication evidence surfaced during dispute
controls.evidence: Chargeback evidence bundles include customer_signature_evidence with IP address + timestamp + device_fingerprint_hash — the SCA proof from checkout. The evidence proves the customer authenticated the transaction at the required strength for the network.

controls.principle: traceable audit trail of every revenue reversal
controls.evidence: Every lost chargeback carries: amount_cents, currency, reason_code, reason_category + TWO linked transaction_ids (disputed amount + network fee) + linked credit_note_id (via invoice::CreditNoteIssuer). Cross-referenced with parent payment + invoice for full revenue-recognition audit. Retained 7 years.

controls.principle: record of tax reversal per jurisdiction
controls.evidence: The parent invoice carries jurisdiction via finance::tax::TaxCalculation. The credit note issued on lost chargeback carries the tax reversal per line. Reduces output VAT / sales tax for the reporting period.

controls.criterion: SOX 404 — internal controls over financial reporting
controls.principle: audit trail on chargeback authorization + reversal
controls.evidence: Every chargeback state transition is Auditable (7-year retention). Evidence submission requires refunds.submit-evidence permission. RDR accept requires refunds.accept + separate chargeback_rdr entitlement. Manual mark-lost requires admin+owner role only. Terms are IMMUTABLE post-terminal. Force-lost restricted to admin+owner + requires 256-char decision_reason for audit trail.

Chargeback CRUD requires finance/admin/owner. Evidence submission requires separate chargebacks.submit-evidence permission. RDR accept requires admin+owner + separate entitlement. Manual mark-lost requires admin+owner + written decision_reason. Confidential financial fields require chargebacks.view-financials permission. Evidence bundles require chargebacks.view-evidence permission (segregated — some support roles need chargeback context but not customer PII drill-down). Platform-plane cross-tenant read requires super_admin/ops/support role.
All routes are TLS-only + require auth:sanctum (tenant) or auth:platform_admin (platform). Provider webhooks verified via payment::PaymentWebhookVerifier signature check.

academorix.chargeback.atomicity.failures_total = 0 target. academorix.chargeback.finalization_failures_total = 0 target. Every ChargebackLost fires the 5-step finalization chain + records metrics; failures dispatch a warn + alert.

academorix.chargeback.rate_threshold_exceeded_total counter — P1 signal on VDMP/MDMP threshold breach. MonitorChargebackRateJob nightly. Platform-plane rate-threshold-alerts endpoint for cross-tenant risk monitoring by Academorix ops.

Cross-chargeback invariant prevents over-charging beyond payment amount. Immutable-after-terminal prevents accidental mutation. Evidence deadline monitoring prevents auto-loss surprises. Rate threshold monitoring prevents merchant-account termination surprises. RDR path provides low-cost defense for small disputes.
Chargeback + ChargebackEvidence rows are Auditable (7-year retention). Every state transition + every field mutation + every reversal audit-logged. Audit-log entries survive cascade deletes.

Two-guard permission surface (sanctum + platform_admin). Confidential financial fields restricted to finance+admin+support+owner. Evidence viewing restricted to chargebacks.view-evidence. RDR accept + manual mark-lost restricted per role.

The chargeback-status-chip + network-badge + reason-code-picker widgets use HeroUI's design tokens which meet AA contrast by default. Status colours never rely on colour alone — each carries a text label.
Chargeback list + detail + evidence submission wizard + RDR accept modal + mark-lost modal are fully keyboard-navigable per HeroUI ComboBox + Modal + Form semantics.

Every observer refusal returns a specific error code (CHARGEBACK_MANAGEMENT_NOT_ENTITLED, CHARGEBACK_INVALID_REASON_CODE, CHARGEBACK_EVIDENCE_DEADLINE_MISSED, CHARGEBACK_IMMUTABLE, etc.) + user-facing message. Refusal responses include actionable reason + support-contact CTA when appropriate.

controls.principle: chargeback rate above 0.9% triggers merchant risk program
controls.evidence: MonitorChargebackRateJob computes rolling 60-day rate per network + fires ChargebackRateThresholdExceeded when rate >= 0.9%. Dispatches P1 admin notification + Academorix platform ops alert. Sustained breaches lead to merchant-account termination — early warning at 75% of threshold gives ops runway to intervene.

controls.principle: chargeback rate above 1.5% triggers merchant risk program
controls.evidence: Same monitoring, different threshold + label.

controls.principle: chargeback rate above 1.0% triggers Amex review
controls.evidence: Same monitoring, different threshold + label.

controls.principle: chargeback rate above 1.0% triggers Discover review
controls.evidence: Same monitoring, different threshold + label.

controls.principle: structured evidence framework for fraud disputes
controls.evidence: Ce3EvidenceBuilder assembles CE3.0-shape evidence (two prior legitimate purchases + matching IP + device fingerprint) alongside standard evidence for eligible fraud chargebacks. Enterprise + Medium tenants only (via chargeback_ce3 entitlement).

controls.principle: auto-refund below threshold to close pre-chargeback state
controls.evidence: RdrHandler creates a Refund via refund::RefundOrchestrator BEFORE the chargeback fully files. No network fee. No rate impact. Enterprise + Medium tenants only (via chargeback_rdr entitlement).

controls.principle: audit trail retained 1y online + 3y total minimum
controls.evidence: Chargeback records retained 7y (10y Enterprise) — exceeds PCI-DSS minimum. Every state transition records the provider_reference_id (token, never PAN) + fee_cents + reason_code.

Chargeback evidence submission observer scans every evidence body for PAN patterns (13-19 digit strings matching card BIN prefixes) + refuses with CHARGEBACK_EVIDENCE_PCI_VIOLATION. Only tokens + last-4 + BIN permitted in evidence. Device fingerprints stored as SHA-256 hashes (never raw).
